title: Project: ~REDLINE~
description: Master high-speed momentum in a grungy, Y2K-inspired cyberpunk metropolis, where every wall-run and slide brings you closer to the truth behind your brother's mysterious disappearance.

title: Day Trading Simulator
description: Welcome to the high-pressure world of the fruit-tech stock market! In Day Trading Simulator, you start with a modest $150 and a dream of becoming a market tycoon. Trade shares in the industry's biggest (and riskiest) players: `Lemon Inc`, `Mango Ltd`, and `Banana Corp`.
developerNotes: Built in C++ using the raylib library, I implemented a approximation of a day trading market using differing layers of perlin noise, random noise, white noise, etc. With those layers set in place, we are able to form a close approximation of the day trading experience by adjust different parameters and implementing random events such as a volatile week, sudden rise and falls by changing the type of noise being used in the current market.

title: Retallica
roles: Game Designer, Rhythm Gameplay Programmer, UI Programmer
description: Rettalica is a rhythm-puzzle game where you play as a metal-themed Pied Piper guiding your squad of rats through weird, twisty industrial mazes. Hit your commands on the beat, lead your rat crew, and figure out how to escape each level without missing a step (or a squeak).
developerNotes: Tasked with implementing differing music combos, I built 3 gameplay layers. Audio syncing, A input buffer / Combo handler, and Dynamic drum generation based on user input. Bringing these three together, we are able to make it so that the player inputs musical drum-breakdowns by timing their input combos according to the background drum layer.

title: Rover - I
awards: #3 Innovation for Global Game Jam 2025 @ CIIT Interweave
description: “Rover I” is a 3D Puzzle/Parkour Game that revolves around using a combination of your parkour skills and certain types of bubbles that augment your ability to traverse the environment.
developerNotes: Apart from the basic movement layer, the player is able to handle the bubble-gun. By making the player shoot different bubble types, I implemented various event handling based on the bubbles the player touches; including the jump bubble, the speed bubble, and the carry bubble which lets you cross large gaps with the caveat being you are unable to course correct.

title: Risk of Fail
description: Deadlines are incoming and assignments are not finished, will you survive this battle for grades and deadlines?
developerNotes: As lead developer for the BYTES club's Risk of Fail, I implemented the core gameplay loop, enemy AI using A* pathfinding, and UI functionalities. I was also tasked with designing some of the levels in the game ensuring a fair layout that promotes replayability through collectibles.

title: Dreams of the past.
description: Go through your old home to find the 5 items important to you whilst making sure to stay safe from the embodiment of death himself, Be careful, He can hear every sound you make from the clicks of your flashlight to doors being swung.
developerNotes: With the theme 'Only 2 Colors,' I helped develop a system for dynamically changing rooms around the player while being pursued by an AI. I also designed the UI, color grading, and room layouts, and modeled props in Blender to enhance the game’s atmosphere.

title: Xtreme Neo Combat
description: As a powerful mech, you must fend off wave after wave of military forces, using a variety of weapons and abilities at your disposal.
developerNotes: For the game jam themed 'Evolve,' I implemented systems to scale enemy frequency and toughness dynamically and designed a rogue-lite progression system for Xtreme Neo Combat.

title: Let's Keep Going
description: A thrilling journey through the stars! Take control of a powerful rocket and fly through the endless void of space.  Except the game gets faster. Rely on your fast reactions and decision making to weave through the endless void of space obstacles!
developerNotes: This game relies on infinite level generation which was achieved by two systems, The terrain generator which relies on generating the next piece by having a set of possible pieces to prevent impossible combinations. This runs recursively giving the sense of infinite level generation. The coin layer acts much simpler but similar at the same time by choosing a random set of coins to spawn in a tile, After that, it checks if said coins are possible to obtain and deletes those that do not fit the terrain tile.

title: Clinic Management System
description: A Clinic Management System designed for a small clinic to efficiently manage patients, appointments, staff shifts, and medical inventory.
developerNotes: Built with React, Flask, and SQLite3, it integrates key data tables to streamline clinic operations and ensure organized, accessible records for improved workflow.

title: Portfolio Website
description: Made in accordance for my finals for my computing class, will also be used as a portfolio website.

title: Attendance Tracker
description: Worked together with a team to design, conceptualize, and develop a new front-end interface for their employee attendance tracker website based on the aesthetics and brand of the company.
